key_insight: MEDA combines LLM-guided reasoning with symbolic regression to automatically discover biologically plausible ODE models, not just fit trajectories.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical workflow for model discovery in systems biology, helping researchers generate mechanistic equations from prior knowledge and data with less manual equation design.
method: Retrieves background knowledge and defines admissible variables for the target biological system.
Generates mechanistic constraints and candidate ODEs using an agentic LLM+SR pipeline.
Fits, evaluates, and refines models across retrieval, extrapolation, and open-ended discovery settings.
evidence_signal: Across canonical retrieval, unseen-variant extrapolation, and open-ended discovery, MEDA recovered correct state variables and showed strong structural recovery, with ablations indicating knowledge-guided constraints were essential.
watch_item: Trajectory-fit quality alone can hide biologically incorrect equations, so the system still depends on reliable priors, constraints, and careful validation.
paper_title: Automatic Ordinary Differential Equations Discovery For Biological Systems Using Large Language Model Powered Agentic System
